decisions.reasoning: Knock Off provides consistent chip damage and removes the opponent's item, which is highly valuable against an Assault Vest Tornadus-Therian. Since I am not in immediate danger of being KO'd, applying pressure is the optimal play.

Flare Blitz is a 2HKO on Tornadus-Therian, and since Incineroar is not in immediate danger of being OHKOed, maximizing damage output is the priority to force the Regenerator user out or secure the KO.
Tornadus-Therian is at 9% HP, meaning Flare Blitz is a guaranteed KO. Securing the KO removes the opponent's Regenerator pivot and puts us at a numerical advantage.
Tornadus-Therian is at 9% HP, meaning Flare Blitz will easily secure the KO regardless of its defensive investment, preventing it from using Regenerator to heal.
Incineroar is at 2% HP and will be KOed by any move from Iron Hands; switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Drain Punch and forces the opponent to respect a faster, high-damage threat.
I outspeed Iron Hands and Moonblast is a guaranteed OHKO, making it the optimal play to remove the threat immediately.

Flutter Mane is threatened with an OHKO by Flare Blitz and cannot secure a KO this turn. Switching into Incineroar allows me to absorb the hit with Intimidate and resist the Fire-type move, preserving Flutter Mane for later.
My Incineroar has fainted, so I must send out a new Pokemon. Flutter Mane is the best choice to pressure the opponent's Incineroar and maintain offensive momentum.

Incineroar threatens an OHKO with Flare Blitz and I cannot secure a KO this turn. Switching to Calyrex-Ice preserves Flutter Mane for later while bringing in a bulkier threat that can handle Incineroar's attacks better.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and the best play to pressure Incineroar. Even if Incineroar attacks, I survive the hit and can finish it off next turn, maintaining momentum.
Calyrex-Ice has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Flutter Mane is the optimal choice to outspeed Incineroar and threaten it with STAB Moonblast.
Incineroar threatens an OHKO with Flare Blitz and I cannot secure a KO this turn. Switching to Dragonite provides a bulky pivot that resists Fire-type moves, allowing me to safely navigate the turn.
